channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Vance’s Trip to Peace Talks on Hold
item.description: Vice President Vance's trip to Pakistan is on hold due to Iran's lack of response to US negotiating positions. The vice president’s trip to Pakistan was suspended because Tehran did not respond to American negotiating positions, a U. S.

item.title: Uncertainty Surrounds U.S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End
item.description: US and Iran talks are uncertain as a cease-fire nears its end. Iran War Live Updates: Uncertainty Surrounds U. S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End The New York Times. Uncertainty Surrounds U. S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End.

item.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
item.description: The US is pressuring Iraq to distance itself from Iran by dismantling Iran-backed militias. Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U. S.

item.title: Deutsche Telekom exploring merger with T-Mobile, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Deutsche Telekom is exploring a merger with T-Mobile, according to Bloomberg News. The potential deal could significantly impact the telecommunications industry. Deutsche Telekom exploring merger with T-Mobile, Bloomberg News reports.

item.title: Vance not yet left for Iran talks, still in Washington to participate in meetings, says White House
item.description: Pakistan's Information Minister said the country has yet to receive a formal response on Iranian participation in talks. Meanwhile, over 11.6 lakh Indians have returned from West Asia amid the conflict.

item.title: 2 Americans Killed in Mexico Crash Were C.I.A. Members
item.description: Two Americans killed in a Mexico crash were CIA members returning from a counter-cartel operation. The incident occurred in Chihuahua state, where Mexico's armed forces were leading the operation. The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.

item.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
item.description: Two US embassy officials who died in a car accident in Mexico's Chihuahua state were reportedly CIA agents. Mexico is investigating a possible breach of its constitution related to the incident.

item.title: Iran won't confirm it will attend peace talks; Trump ready to resume bombing ahead of ceasefire
item.description: Iran's attendance at peace talks is unconfirmed, while US President Trump is prepared to resume bombing ahead of a ceasefire deadline. The situation remains uncertain as the deadline approaches. Live updates: Iran won't confirm it will attend peace talks; Trump ready to resume bombing ahead of ceasefire deadline NBC News.

item.title: Flu vaccine no longer mandatory for soldiers, says US military chief
item.description: The US military has lifted its mandatory flu vaccine requirement for soldiers. The decision was made on the principle of "medical autonomy," according to military chief Pete Hegseth. Pete Hegseth says the decision is based on principle of 'medical autonomy' and criticises the mandate as 'overreaching'.

item.title: Fired U.K. Official Describes ‘Pressure’ to Expedite Envoy’s Appointment
item.description: A fired UK official claimed Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office pressured them to quickly appoint Peter Mandelson, associated with Jeffrey Epstein, as ambassador to Washington. Mandelson's swift installation was sought.

item.title: Two Palestinians Killed in Israeli Shooting Near West Bank School
item.description: Israeli forces killed two Palestinians, including a child, near a West Bank school in Al Mughayir. The Israeli military said they intervened to break up clashes amid a surge in settler violence.

item.title: The US exit from Syria, explained
item.description: The US withdrew from Syria after 10 years, impacting Kurdish forces and regional tensions. The move raises questions about the country's future and the balance of power in the region.

item.title: Magyar says Netanyahu will face arrest in Hungary
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu may face arrest in Hungary due to an ICC arrest warrant issued in 2024. The warrant cites allegations of crimes against humanity and war crimes.

item.title: Trump says U.S. in ‘very, very strong’ position for Iran talks
item.description: US President Trump stated the US is in a "very, very strong" position for Iran talks. He suggested Iran could improve chances of successful peace talks by freeing eight women facing execution.

item.title: What’s the bill to rebuild Gaza, and who will pay?
item.description: A UN-EU report estimates $71 billion is needed over 10 years to rebuild Gaza. The report highlights the substantial financial requirement for reconstruction efforts. UN-EU report says $71bn needed over 10 years.

item.title: Uncertainty swirls around US-Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: Uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks as a ceasefire deadline looms. The talks' outcome remains unclear, with tensions between the two nations high. Live updates: Uncertainty swirls around US-Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms CNN.

item.title: Ex-official describes pressure from U.K. PM's office, deepening Mandelson row
item.description: U. K. Prime Minister's office allegedly applied pressure on a former official to justify the appointment of Peter Mandelson, deepening the controversy surrounding the decision. Starmer has since expressed regret over the appointment.

item.title: Cuba confirms recent talks with visiting U.S. delegation
item.description: Cuba confirmed recent talks with a visiting U. S. delegation, which outlined conditions for continued negotiations, including the release of prominent political prisoners. The U. negotiators presented their demands to the Cuban government.

item.title: Amnesty warns that 'predator' leaders seek to impose new 'world order'
item.description: Amnesty International Secretary General Agnes Callamard criticized US President Donald Trump, Russia's Vladimir Putin, and Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu as "predators" seeking to impose a new world order. She condemned their actions and policies.

item.title: Israeli sexual violence helping push Palestinians from West Bank: Report
item.description: A report by an NGO found at least 16 cases of conflict-related sexual violence against Palestinians in the West Bank, attributed to Israeli settlers and soldiers. This has contributed to the displacement of Palestinians from the region.
